channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Key Controversies and Career Highlights of Louise Arbour as Governor General
item.description: Louise Arbour, recently appointed as Canada's next governor general, brings a long and sometimes controversial career in law, human rights, and public service. As a former Supreme Court justice, she s...

item.title: Moi Forces Academy Nairobi Fees Structure and Payment Details Explained
item.description: Moi Forces Academy Nairobi, a public national boys’ secondary school, charges an estimated annual fee of about KSh 53,554, which works out to roughly KSh 17,851 per term. These fees are aligned with M...

item.title: Kenya Assures Public as Temporary Fuel Shortages Hit Some Stations
item.description: Nairobi – The Kenyan government has confirmed that a few petrol stations across the country are experiencing temporary fuel shortages. Energy and Petroleum CS Opiyo Wandayi explained that the disrupti...

item.title: Lacson alleges Marcoleta received allocable funds but avoids accusing him of wrongdoing
item.description: Senator Ping Lacson recently made statements regarding Representative Rodante Marcoleta, claiming that Marcoleta received certain allocable funds linked to government projects. Lacson, however, was ca...

item.title: Authorities Propose Water Sports at Simly Dam Amid Environmental Concerns
item.description: Islamabad authorities, including the Capital Development Authority (CDA), are considering development of water sports and recreational facilities near Simly Dam, a primary source of clean drinking wat...

item.title: U.S. Stock Markets Rally on Hopes for U.S.-Iran Conflict Resolution
item.description: U.S. stock markets surged on May 6, 2026, following reports that the United States and Iran were nearing an agreement to resolve the ongoing conflict. The S&P 500 rose 1.46% to 7,365.12, reaching a re...

item.title: Sindh rolls out Rs14bn early childhood programme with quarterly cash support for caregivers
item.description: The Sindh government has launched a major Early Childhood Development (ECD) programme with a budget of Rs14 billion, aimed at supporting caregivers of children up to five years old. Under this initiat...

item.title: How tobacco companies influence smoking policy in Australia through donations, lobbying and secrecy
item.description: The article examines how tobacco companies continue to influence smoking laws in Australia, despite strong public health frameworks. It contrasts the United Kingdom’s recent move to create a “smokefre...

item.title: Johannesburg firms adapt to new China zero-tariff policy, boosting high-value exports
item.description: Johannesburg is seeing a significant shift in trade dynamics with China following the removal of tariffs on imports from 53 African countries, effective May 1, 2026. For South African businesses, part...

item.title: WNBA enters 30th season with expansion teams, new labour deal, and Aces leading title race
item.description: The WNBA is set to begin its 30th season with several major developments shaping both its business and competitive landscape. A newly negotiated collective bargaining agreement, described as transform...

item.title: China Affirms Iran's Right to Peaceful Nuclear Program Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: During talks in Beijing, Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi reiterated China's support for Iran's right to develop nuclear energy for peaceful purposes, while commending Tehran for its commitment not to...

item.title: Dave Chappelle Reflects on Media Backlash Over Transgender Jokes in Interview with Michelle Obama
item.description: Comedian Dave Chappelle spoke candidly about the media backlash he faced over his past transgender-related jokes in a recent podcast with former first lady Michelle Obama and her brother Craig Robinso...

item.title: Starmer reassures civil servants amid scrutiny over Mandelson vetting
item.description: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has addressed civil servants across Whitehall, emphasising the importance of 'speaking truth to power' following a period of intense scrutiny in the Foreign Office. His...

item.title: Police report mutual complaints after Quezon City road rage dispute
item.description: In Quezon City, authorities have confirmed that both parties involved in a recent road rage incident have filed complaints against each other. The incident reportedly involved a bus and a motorcycle, ...

item.title: Pakistan Armed Forces Reaffirm Readiness and Strength on Marka-i-Haq Anniversary
item.description: On the first anniversary of the 2025 conflict with India, dubbed 'Marka-i-Haq' by the state, Pakistan's Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR) reaffirmed that any hostile design against the country wi...

item.title: Guide to Welsh Parliament 2026 Election: Party Policies and Key Issues
item.description: On 7 May 2026, Wales will hold elections for the Senedd Cymru, the Welsh Parliament, which will expand from 60 to 96 seats. The BBC has prepared a comprehensive guide to help voters understand the pol...

item.title: India's Table Tennis Teams Exit Early at World Championships
item.description: India’s participation at the 2026 World Table Tennis Team Championships concluded on a disappointing note with both men’s and women’s teams exiting in the round of 32. The women’s team faced the Unite...

item.title: APC Clears President Tinubu From Screening Ahead Of 2027 Primaries
item.description: Di All Progressives Congress (APC) don officially waive di screening requirement for President Bola Ahmed Tinubu. Dis one mean say President Tinubu fit participate for di party's 2027 presidential pri...

item.title: Migrant Workers Face Systemic Exploitation in Australia: Findings from a 10,000-Person Survey
item.description: A comprehensive survey of nearly 10,000 temporary visa holders in Australia, conducted by the Migrant Justice Institute, reveals that workplace exploitation is widespread rather than isolated. The stu...

item.title: Proposed CGT reforms may significantly impact start-up ownership and investment
item.description: Recent discussions around potential changes to Australia's capital gains tax (CGT) could have far-reaching effects on start-ups and private capital investment. The government is reportedly considering...
